body:
**Verified on `main` @ `82be6ae`.** `crates/core/src/repair.rs` enforces the load-bearing invariant on **both** producers it owns: `fragment_intact` (`repair.rs:53-54`) and `intact_shard` (`:64-68`) accept a fragment only if it decodes cleanly **and** `decoded.header.chunk_id == chunk`. The doc comment at `:50-51` explicitly claims *"the read path decodes for the same effect inline (`crates/core/src/read.rs`)"*.

**But the read path does not.** `read.rs:138` (single-fragment / `None` scheme) and `read.rs:176` (ReedSolomon) call `decode(&fragment)` and accept on `Ok` — verifying only the self-describing **checksum**, never `header.chunk_id == chunk`. A misplaced-but-intact fragment (correct checksum, wrong `chunk_id` — e.g. a misrouted/placement-confused fragment) is therefore:
- returned directly as the chunk payload (`None` scheme), or
- pushed as a shard at `index` into the ReedSolomon decoder → silent corrupt reconstruction.

Scrub/repair would catch and re-enqueue this fragment; the read path silently trusts it. The asymmetry contradicts the invariant the module documents as *shared*.

**Suggested fix.** Have the read path use the shared `core::repair` verify (`intact_shard` / `fragment_intact`) instead of bare `decode`, so the `chunk_id` check is enforced on read too.

**Refs:** #143 (read/repair), #144 (M3.6); proposal 0005 §174-176, §262-267.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: Read path skips the chunk_id recheck that scrub/repair enforce — a misplaced-but-intact fragment is silently decoded
